entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.13
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.13 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q3 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.409 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Software Center.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Data Audit: Ability to delete audit data based on other filters | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Disable the welcome email for new users | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • AI for Planning and Analytics
    • Just Ask Comparison | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Data Exploration
    • Data Analyzer - Share an insight from within the tool
    • Data Analyzer - Shortcut Link | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Calculations Dialog: LOG, LOG10, CEIL, ROUND, TRUNC
    • Context Menu Enhancements in Builder Panel / Available Objects list
    • More Filter Members Capabilities (Filter with Swap/Remove; Contextual Highlighting for Tuple Filter) | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Enterprise Planning
    • Data Actions - Mapping Strategy "Property" | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Multi Actions – creation and update by BI users | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Microsoft Office Integration
    • Frontend Calculations referencing members
    • Null Suppression
  • Story Design
    • Smart Grouping in Charts in the optimized story experience
    • Public URL API support for BW Hierarchies
  • Story Extensibility
    • Widget Customization Add-on to support legend | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Create calculated or account-based dimensions in custom widgets as charts and tables | (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Administration and Service Management

 

Data Audit: Ability to delete audit data based on other filters

When deleting data changes to reduce the size of your data change log, you can now add filters on dimensions of the model, as well as Audit Time, Audit User, and Audit Action, to control which data changes to delete. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Disable the welcome email for new users

You can now disable the welcome email from being sent to new users added to you tenant. For more information, see Manage the Welcome Email Notification. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

AI for Planning and Analytics

 

Just Ask Comparison

You can now use the following in Just Ask questions: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

  • Compare two measures: Compare sales with expenses
  • Compare two measures and a dimension: Compare sales with expenses by location
  • Time comparisons: Compare sales with expenses by location in current year
  • Combine a comparison and a calculation: Compare average sales by location with average expenses by location this year
  • Combine a version dimension with a measure comparison: Compare actual sales with forecast in Canada

 

Data Exploration

 

Data Analyzer - Share an insight from within the tool

You can now share an insight right from data analyzer by using the function Share Insight in the new Share toolbar button. In the Share Insight dialog you can add the users or teams with whom you’d like to share the insight, set the access type and set if new recipients should get an email.

 
Data Analyzer - Shortcut Link

If you want to capture the state of the current view on your data in data analyzer without saving it, you can now create a shortcut link by using the function Create Shortcut Link in the new Share button in the in the toolbar. The generated shortcut link can be copied to the clipboard and thus be added for example to an email or opened in a new tab. A cleanup task is scheduled in the system configuration area to delete shortcut links in recurrent period. As an administrator you can reconfigure the default period. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Calculations Dialog: LOG, LOG10, CEIL, ROUND, TRUNC

We have introduced support for two mathematical and three conversion functions:

  • LOG(number) and LOG10(number) compute the natural logarithm and the base-10 logarithm of a positive number, respectively.
  • CEIL returns a real number that is greater or equal to the entered number
  • ROUND rounds to a specified number of decimal places.
  • TRUNC truncates the provided number at the specified decimal place.

These new functions enhance data manipulation in both BW and HANA systems, allowing for more precise and advanced arrangements of data. 

Categorization of functions:

  • Functions are now grouped under the following categories - Conversion, Logical, Date and Time, Mathematical, Lookup & Reference, Business, and Dynamic Time. 

  • Added a new search box in the function's sections for quicker access to functions, maintaining category clarity during search.

The new categorized layout with search capabilities enhances user experience and streamlines the generation of calculations.

 

Context Menu Enhancements in Builder Panel / Available Objects list

In data analyzer multiple enhancements have been added to improve the user workflow:

  • Added splitter and the Expand/Collapse button ensure a better space management in the data analyzer Builder panel.
  • In the Available Objects list you can now use the More menu with filter functions.
  • In the Available Objects list there you can now work with a context menu by using the right-mouse click function.
  • In the Available Objects list now all members of the accounts/measures are listed, not only the booked

 

More Filter Members Capabilities (Filter with Swap/Remove; Contextual Highlighting for Tuple Filter)

Table context menu enhanced with extra filter options: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

Filter Member and >

  • Swap Dimension With > then this context menu offers the user to Apply Filter members with this selection and Swap with another dimension to be placed on Axis
  • Remove Dimension{}then this context menu offers the user to apply Filter members with this selection and remove the dimension(s) from Axis.
  • Filter color Highlighter - The color highlighter with different filter options (Filter Member, Filter, Exclude Member, Exclude, Filter Members)

 

Enterprise Planning

 

Data Actions - Mapping Strategy "Property"

In the cross model copy step one can now select the property which is used for the Identical names mapping. With this feature the user can map automatically dimensions by maintaining an additional property in the modeler

 

Multi Actions – creation and update by BI users

The standard "BI Admin" role contains multi actions privileges. Users with a BI license have the capability to create and execute multi actions that include BI steps only (excluding BI embedded license). Additionally, the names of the action triggers, such as multi action trigger, data action trigger, and BPC planning sequence trigger, have changed and they are now called starters: multi action starter, data action starter, and BPC planning sequence starter.

 

Microsoft Office Integration

 

Frontend Calculations referencing members

In your front-end calculations, you can now reference dimension members and properties.

This enables you to combine SAP Analytics Cloud and Excel features as you can use XLOOKUP, IF, and more Excel features in your formulas.

This feature is available to customers as of June 19, 2024.

 

Null Suppression

You now have the option Null Suppression to suppress null values (-) in a table. The option is available in the context menu on Rows or Columns.

With the existing option Zero Suppression, you can suppress zero (0) and null (-) values in one step.

This feature is available to customers as of June 19, 2024.

 

Story Design

 

Smart Grouping in Charts in the optimized story experience

You can now apply Smart Grouping to Charts in the optimized story experience.

 
Public URL API support for BW Hierarchies

The SAP Analytics Cloud URL API allows you to filter on time and Parent child Hierarchies which are not default for BW Live Data connections. fXXHierarchy  parameter supports to filter on time and parent-child hierarchies with different hierarchal values.  

This functionality allows developers to generate URLs for stories with embedded hierarchy node filters, enhancing the end-user experience. Users will benefit from pre-filtered stories that present data specific to particular hierarchy nodes.

Parameter Syntax: f<XX>Hierarchy=<HierarchyName>

  • <XX> is any number between 01 and 99. If there are several filters passed for filtering then assign a unique value of <XX> to each filter. <HierarchyName> is the name of the hierarchy you want to filter on.

This is only supported in the Optimized Story Experience.

 

Story Extensibility

 

Widget Customization Add-on to support legend

Users can now develop their own widget add-ons for customizing chart legends. This new capability enhances the overall storytelling experience of data representation. Supported chart types are bar/column, stacked bar/column, stacked area and line. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

Refer to SAP Analytics Cloud Widget Add-On Developer Guide for more.

 

Create calculated or account-based dimensions in custom widgets as charts and tables

In the optimized story experience, you can create and utilize calculated or account-based dimensions for custom widgets. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases.

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. And check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: House Keeping activities in a non cumulative ADSO (Inventory)
entry.content:
Hi guys.

I am in the process of carrying out maintenance activities on the inventory cube, as you well know this cube is non-cumulative.

I would like to do one delete per day but in transaction DELETE_FACTS I don't see the 0CALDAY feature.

Is there a better way to purge information from a non-cumulative cube?

Thanks in advance.

PS. I forgto to mention that I'm working on BW/4HANA 2.0 SP11

entry.title: Re: Service Order with Advance Shipment of Spare Parts
entry.content:
Hello @GertTackaert 

Thanks a lot for the detailed blog. How can we handle the Unused spare part return? Assuming I am ordering to supplier 5 Quantities of Motor via Advance shipment, PR is Generated to Supplier and after PO and GR, I have got the Consignment stock of 5Pc in my Consignment sales order. 

Now the Technician went to the Site, dismantled the Defective Motor and used only 2PCs from the Consignment order; Completed the Service. What is the procedure to return this Unused 3Pc Motors? Can we use Advance returns Management process for return of Unused spare part in the consignment stock back to Storage location or to Supplier? What is your recommendation?

And one more requirement is, how can we return the Defective Motors which the technician dismantled from the Machine; This also belongs to the same Service order?

Many thanks.

Girish

entry.content:
Our exclusive Temu coupon codes{frp195176} or{frf195176} gives you a flat $100 off your purchase, plus an additional 70% discount on top of that. New Temu customers can save up to 70% using the code {frp195176} or{frf195176}. Existing users can enjoy a 40% discount on their next purchase with code {frp195176} or{frf195176}.

For businesses operating in Brazil, navigating the complexities of tax regulations can be a challenge. A crucial aspect of this is complying with electronic invoicing, mandated by the Nota Fiscal Eletronica (NF-e) system. This article explores SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica, a solution designed to simplify NF-e compliance for SAP users.

Understanding NF-e
NF-e refers to the electronic invoice format used in Brazil for transactions involving goods. It eliminates paper invoices, promoting tax efficiency and streamlining the invoicing process. Companies must generate NF-e in a specific XML format, containing details like buyer and seller information, product specifics, and tax data.

SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica: A Helping Hand
SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica is a software solution offered by SAP that aids businesses in complying with NF-e regulations. It integrates seamlessly with existing SAP systems, automating manual tasks and ensuring accurate invoice generation.

Key Benefits of SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica
Enhanced Compliance: The solution automates NF-e generation in the mandated XML format, minimizing errors and ensuring compliance with Brazilian tax authorities (SEFAZ).
Streamlined Processes: Manual data entry for invoices is significantly reduced, saving time and resources.
Improved Accuracy: Automating invoice creation reduces the risk of errors associated with manual data entry.
Secure Communication: Secure and certified communication with SEFAZ is facilitated through the solution.
Efficiency Gains: Faster invoice processing translates to quicker settlements and improved cash flow.
Important Note: Upcoming End of Maintenance"

It's important to be aware that SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica (SAP NFE) is an on-premise solution nearing its end-of-maintenance date (December 31, 2025). SAP recommends exploring alternative solutions to ensure continued compliance with evolving NF-e regulations.

entry.title: Re: Fiori List Report displays identical records despite underlying CDS View containing distinct dat
entry.content:

If you are using transaction SEGW for a gateway service, go to the properties of the entity types and set the keys correctly according to the CDS views.

entry.title: Re: HANA restore delta without log backups
entry.content:
You are mixing concepts regarding INCREMENTAL and DIFFERENTIAL backups. According to your image, you have scheduled daily INCREMENTAL backups, meaning that to restore FRIDAY backup you will need 6 backups: the latest SATURDAY FULL and all INCREMENTALS. If you scheduled DIFFERENTIAL, only 2 will be needed.

SAP HANA backup help is here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/c4663eabbb571014923a90c70cec566c.html

Incremental backup saves the data changed since the last data backup while differential backups save all the data changed since the last full data backup. Delta backups enable you to optimize your backup strategy, for example by less frequently executing the more time-consuming full data backups. Compared to log backups, delta backups can reduce the time needed for a recovery as the amount of log that needs to be replayed is reduced.

Log backups can bring the database to a more recent point-in-time, to recover the changes made since the latest data backup recovered.

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve Employee data using Compound Employee...
entry.content:
Hey, i was in the same issue, solved with this SOAP request:

 

<soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:urn="urn:sfobject.sfapi.successfactors.com">
<soapenv:Header/>
<soapenv:Body>
<urn:query>
<urn:queryString><![CDATA[SELECT person,identity_information,personal_information,address_information,phone_information,email_information,person_relation,employment_information,job_information,job_relation,national_id_card,dependent_information,personal_documents_information FROM CompoundEmployee WHERE LAST_MODIFIED_ON > to_datetime('2024-06-01T00:00:00Z')]]></urn:queryString>
<!--Zero or more repetitions:-->
<urn:param>
<urn:name>maxRows</urn:name>
<urn:value>${property.lv_maxRows}</urn:value>
</urn:param>
<urn:param>
<urn:name>query mode</urn:name>
<urn:value>delta</urn:value>
</urn:param>
</urn:query>
</soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

and, yes, you must get a session id before:

<soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:urn="urn:sfobject.sfapi.successfactors.com">
<soapenv:Header/>
<soapenv:Body>
<urn:login>
<urn:credential>
<urn:companyId>${property.lv_SFEC_Company}</urn:companyId>
<urn:username>${property.lvUsername}</urn:username>
<urn:password>${property.lvPassword}</urn:password>
</urn:credential>
<!--Zero or more repetitions:-->
</urn:login>
</soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

 

Regards

Enhanced Compliance: The solution automates NF-e generation in the mandated XML format, minimizing errors and ensuring compliance with Brazilian tax authorities (SEFAZ).
Streamlined Processes: Manual data entry for invoices is significantly reduced, saving time and resources.
Improved Accuracy: Automating invoice creation reduces the risk of errors associated with manual data entry.
Secure Communication: Secure and certified communication with SEFAZ is facilitated through the solution.
Efficiency Gains: Faster invoice processing translates to quicker settlements and improved cash flow.
Important Note: Upcoming End of Maintenance"

It's important to be aware that SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica (SAP NFE) is an on-premise solution nearing its end-of-maintenance date (December 31, 2025). SAP recommends exploring alternative solutions to ensure continued compliance with evolving NF-e regulations.

For businesses operating in Brazil, navigating the complexities of tax regulations can be a challenge. A crucial aspect of this is complying with electronic invoicing, mandated by the Nota Fiscal Eletronica (NF-e) system. This article explores SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica, a solution designed to simplify NF-e compliance for SAP users.

Understanding NF-e
NF-e refers to the electronic invoice format used in Brazil for transactions involving goods. It eliminates paper invoices, promoting tax efficiency and streamlining the invoicing process. Companies must generate NF-e in a specific XML format, containing details like buyer and seller information, product specifics, and tax data.

SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica: A Helping Hand
SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica is a software solution offered by SAP that aids businesses in complying with NF-e regulations. It integrates seamlessly with existing SAP systems, automating manual tasks and ensuring accurate invoice generation.

Key Benefits of SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica
Enhanced Compliance: The solution automates NF-e generation in the mandated XML format, minimizing errors and ensuring compliance with Brazilian tax authorities (SEFAZ).
Streamlined Processes: Manual data entry for invoices is significantly reduced, saving time and resources.
Improved Accuracy: Automating invoice creation reduces the risk of errors associated with manual data entry.
Secure Communication: Secure and certified communication with SEFAZ is facilitated through the solution.
Efficiency Gains: Faster invoice processing translates to quicker settlements and improved cash flow.
Important Note: Upcoming End of Maintenance"

It's important to be aware that SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica (SAP NFE) is an on-premise solution nearing its end-of-maintenance date (December 31, 2025). SAP recommends exploring alternative solutions to ensure continued compliance with evolving NF-e regulations.

entry.title: Manager search field for assignment profile rule
entry.content:
We have an assignment profile containing users who work in CA or their WAH state is CA, to assign specific state-required training. I also want to dynamically assign the managers of that group of employees living in California. It doesn't matter where the manager resides, but I need to identify if they have California employees who report to them. I thought about creating a custom field for the managers but it would require another integration step because the data goes from BizX to Workday and then to SF LMS. I prefer the AP method, but if necessary, we might have to choose to add curriculum to the managers via the learning management tool instead. anybody had a similar need and solution?

entry.title: To RISE or Not To RISE
entry.content:
This is a key decision facing most organisations that run their SAP estate in an on-premise environment or natively on a hyperscaler and are thinking about the future.

It is not an easy decision to make and requires deep analysis relating to their current operating model, the resource and skill mix available and SAP’s future roadmap and strategy. Organizations also need to identify the expected operational, commercial and process benefits to justify the move to RISE and change their status quo.

Having been associated with RISE with SAP since its inception, I have seen how SAP’s offering has matured over the years. SAP is making significant investments into the operational and delivery capabilities of RISE. SAP’s strategy has also evolved to fully support this cloud model. It is my firm belief that NOW is the right time for customers to decide and move to RISE with SAP. Here are some of the reasons.

 

Maturity

RISE with SAP was launched by SAP’s CEO, Christian Klein in January 2021. Since then, the offering has matured significantly. The commercialisation of a large proportion of SAP’s  previously on-premise solution offerings has occurred to allow them to now be offered in the cloud model.

There has been significant investment in tools, automation and people so that the delivery of the cloud service is world class. The ‘Enterprise Cloud Services’ or ECS organisation is the delivery arm of SAP for the RISE offering where these investments have occurred.

ECS supports the operational delivery & support of the RISE offering and is separate to the implementation services which implements S/4HANA. ECS is purely involved in the operational, day-to-day, business-as-usual (BAU) maintenance and management of customer’s SAP landscape under RISE.

 

New Board Area - Customer Services & Delivery

At the start of 2024, a new board area was established called, ‘Customer Services & Delivery’ which brings together all the resources involved in the architecture and delivery of RISE and associated services. This board area now has architecture and pre-sales teams involved in architecting our customer’s cloud solutions (Cloud architects), the services teams that implement the S/4HANA capabilities (Services teams) and the teams involved in the actual day-to-day operational delivery of service for RISE customers (ECS team).

This was a strategic decision and is extremely important as it reflects the internal transformation within SAP as it expands from an engineering, product development, and a sales organisation, into a cloud service provider ensuring customer success through adoption and delivery of cloud services and focuses on achieving successful customer outcomes.

Our customers will gain from RISE with SAP by utilising SAP’s expertise and experience in managing S/4HANA customers globally and benefiting from SAP’s focus on customer adoption and outcome driven approach.

You can read about these here.

 

Incentives

Since RISE with SAP launched, SAP has been listening to customer feedback and has released the RISE with SAP Migration and Modernisation program in Jan 2024. The news article is here.

Through this program SAP recognises the complexity of the transformation required and offers to actively support customers on their journey to the cloud and to help make the transition easier.

The program offers customers a Migration Methodology to provide guidance across their migration journey, providing roadmaps for innovations, and more significantly, it offers commercial incentives for customers moving to RISE.

This incentive is called the RISE Transformation Incentive and is a limited time fund through till the end of 2024 that SAP has created to support customers moving to RISE. Through this program, SAP customers can leverage this fund and receive incentives in the form of credits as a means of offsetting some of the costs involved in their transformation journeys.

This incentive will be offered to current SAP S/4HANA and SAP ECC on-premise customers moving their SAP landscape to RISE and they will stand to gain credits as a percentage of the amount of their RISE with SAP subscription. These credits can be utilized to offset either the on-premise maintenance costs, implementation services costs or the cost of the RISE subscription fee itself.

To date I have engaged with several customers that have benefited from this incentive. It is my view that this incentive fund represents a significant opportunity for customers to initiate their move to RISE in 2024.  

 

Partner Ecosystem

When RISE was first launched, our partner ecosystem was quite apprehensive. However, over time, our partners have also understood that RISE with SAP is at the core of the SAP Cloud Strategy and have now jointly worked with SAP to tailor their own offerings based on the RISE implementation methodologies.

Several of SAP’s partners have also become RISE customers including Accenture, IBM, Wipro, HCL, Microsoft, which further goes to show the strength of the offering and the trust that partners, most of whom have their own SAP practices, bestow upon SAP in managing their SAP landscapes for them.

 

So, if you are sitting on the fence and trying to decide whether the time is right to move to RISE, I would say that 2024 is definitely the year you should make the decision and take the plunge. I can safely say that you will get the kind of support from SAP that you would have never seen before!

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting 新機能ロードマップ強化のご案内
entry.content:
SAP Financial Consolidation（以降FC）は、長年にわたり広くSAPの連結会計ソリューションとして、多くのお客様にご利用頂いています。一方2017年には、新たな連結会計ソリューションとしてSAP S/4HANAに統合されたSAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting（以降GR）がリリースされており、お客様により高い価値を提供する為の開発が進められています。（GRに関する詳細はこちらをご確認ください） 

本記事では、FCで好評を頂いていた機能の反映を含めた、GRに関する今後の重要な新機能ロードマップについてご紹介させて頂きます。 

SAPでは、FCをご利用のお客様からのフィードバック等も踏まえてGRの新機能ロードマップを加速するプログラムを進めており、連結会計データ収集のためのGroup Reporting Data Collection(以降GRDC)の領域も含めた大きな投資を行っています。 

  • GRDCは、Webフォーム経由で個社報告データを直接入力したり、アップロードするためのクラウドソリューションであり、GRの1コンポーネントとして提供しています。GRDCを中心としたデータ収集領域では今後、データチェック機能やデータインポート＆マッピング機能といった既存機能を入力フォーム画面から起動可能とする新機能や、複数期間データの入力フォームの作成、入力フォーム間のリンク機能、新しいユーザーインターフェースでのデータ収集プロセスモニタリング、パッケージの提出/統合機能など、多数の機能を提供予定です。 
  • 個社/連結調整のためのマニュアル修正仕訳入力画面では、貸借形式での仕訳入力に対応し、より分かり易い操作感を実現します。 
  • 自動連結修正仕訳機能については、FCで好評であった強力で柔軟性の高い連結ルール機能に追加機能を取り込み、新たな連結ルールエンジンとして開発を進めています。 
  • レポーティングにおいては、連結財務諸表作成プロセスにおける担当者間での連携と分析をより円滑にするための、レビューブックレットでの連結データへのコメント入力機能やMicrosoft Teamsとの統合が予定されています。 
  • さらに、連結決算プロセスにおけるAIシナリオとして、財務データの分析・レポーティングに生成AIを組み込むことで、子会社及びグループレベルデータに対する分析コメントを作成する取り組みなどを行っています。 

各機能の詳細については、以下のロードマップ情報リンクからご確認いただけます。 

  • Launch your controls from the package（パッケージからのデータチェック実行） 
  • Enter data in multiple periods（複数期間データ入力フォームの作成） 
  • Import data directly from the package（パッケージからのデータインポートの実行） 
  • Link documents （入力フォーム間のリンク機能） 
  • Monitor the progress of data collection in a completely modernized UI（新UIによるデータ収集プロセスモニタ） 
  • Ability to enter or load reported data for dimension totals (ディメンション合計に対するデータ入力) 
  • Debit-Credit mode presentation of manual journal entries（マニュアル仕訳入力機能の改善） 
  • New consolidation rule engine （自動連結修正仕訳エンジンの刷新） 
  • Create and manage your financial statements more simply（レビューブックレット機能の強化） 
  • Generative AI （SAPの生成AI関連情報全般のご紹介） 
  • Generative AI in the consolidation process（連結会計プロセスにおける自然言語処理アシスタント機能） 
  • Generative AI in the analysis and reporting of financial data（連結データ分析の自動ガイド機能） 

Group Reportingのロードマップ全般に関する情報は以下のリンクからご確認いただけます。 
 SAP Roadmap Explorer.

entry.title: Re: Is there an API Endpoint to attach a file to a specif...
entry.content:
Hi @vrjadhav ,

The API to facilitate the integration for uploading attachments to a quote in a customer project has not been officially published. However, the /Cart/UploadCartAttachment endpoint is utilized by the standard CPQ UI frontend for user interaction execution. This is a stateful API and relies on the X-CSRF-Token for quote context.

To invoke this API via your custom scripts, you'd need to obtain an X-CSRF-Token initially from an open target quote. Considering the different implementation approaches:

  • With API invocation through a Custom Responsive Template, you could directly retrieve the cookie information with the X-CSRF-Token from the user session.
  • If you're calling the API asynchronously like custom background jobs or external system integration, you'd first need to emulate user login via /api/rd/v1/Core/LogIn and open the target quote in Edit mode via /api/rd/v1/QuoteList/ExecuteAction. Remember, it is essential to use the same X-CSRF-Token across the process flow when invoking the stateful APIs to ensure all operations are based on the same quote context.

With respect to your specific use case, I recommend submitting an enhancement request in SAP CPQ | Customer Influence Portal to the SAP CPQ product team. This way, the development team can thoroughly analyze your business case and consider any possible recommendations or future improvements to better support your needs.

Best Regards.
Fancy

entry.title: Update Solman JAVA and Solman Abap separately
entry.content:
Hello Team,

 

Recently SPS19 for SAP solution manager was released. We may not be able to do a complete ABAP+JAVA Update in one go.

I wanted to enquire , if we can update Solman JAVA to SPS19 first then after 3-4 months proceed to Solman ABAP.

Would this have any impact on the functioning of the Solman environment. We use solution manager for SLD, PDF generation ,Monitoring and CHARM.

We will be very thankful for your guidance.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Update Solman JAVA and Solman Abap separately
entry.content:
Hi,

 

We do not recommend this. When you plan to upgrade SAP Solution Manager system, you need to upgrade both ABAP and Java systems to the same SPS level. Please do not update any individual software component to higher SP level without SAP confirmation. It may cause unexpected errors.

Latest Note for SOLMAN 720 SP19: 3377498 - SAP Solution Manager 7.2 SP Stack 19: recommended corrections.

 

Best regards,

Sylas

entry.title: table partition for 2 billion crossed table required steps to change 8 partitions from the default 4
entry.content:
we have observed when checking alerts on the PERSON_NAME Production System Tenant BPP.  Based on the below, the table in the alert won’t be able to accept any more inserts, so that will impact processing.  Things may have changed, but I believe re-partitioning—in this case to 8 partitions from the default 4—is an offline action, would like to know the pre steps for the partition and guidance to proceed further

 

attached table details 

SAPABAP1./BIC/FZ****_RPT partition (4) contains PHONE_*** records. A table partition cannot contain more than 2,147,483,648 (2 billion) rows.

entry.title: Re: table partition for 2 billion crossed table required...

Since this is /BIC/F* table, you could refer to note below for data deletion.
- note
3232938 - [BW Central KBA] How-To: Data Management for BW Infoprovider tables

Regarding the partition, you may need perform a distribution in this BW case, note below contains detailed steps:
- note
2143736 - FAQ: SAP HANA Table Distribution for BW
-> 7. How to perform table redistribution and partitioning on BW on HANA?

Following note contains more details about Redistribution.

- SAP BW/4HANA system
2334091 - SAP BW/4HANA: Table Placement and Landscape Redistribution

- SAP BW on SAP HANA system
1908075 - SAP BW on SAP HANA: Table Placement and Landscape Redistribution

entry.title: Re: Hi I am having an issue with the drop down menu (Buil...
entry.content:
Hello Jack.

The annotations.cds file should have this code relevant to the value help.  You may want to open it up and check ( it should be under the "app" folder in your project ).

annotate service.Risks with {
miti @Common.Text : {
$value : miti.descr,
![@UI.TextArrangement] : #TextOnly,
}
};
annotate service.Risks with {
miti @(Common.ValueList : {
$Type : 'Common.ValueListType',
CollectionPath : 'Mitigations',
Parameters : [
{
$Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut',
LocalDataProperty : miti_ID,
ValueListProperty : 'ID',
},
],
Label : 'Mitigation',
},
Common.ValueListWithFixedValues : true
)};
annotate service.Mitigations with {
ID @Common.Text : descr
};
annotate service.Mitigations with {
owner @Common.FieldControl : #ReadOnly
};
annotate service.Mitigations with {
timeline @Common.FieldControl : #ReadOnly
};

entry.title: Re: SAP for Me: EWA on demand
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

Please note that the EWA reports only include data from the previous whole week, regardless of when the report is generated. The Solution Manager system provides instructions on how to generate an Ad-Hoc EWA report in KBA 2357073. However, unlike SAP Solution Manager, SAP Focused Run does not have the capability to generate Ad-Hoc EWA reports because it does not process the EarlyWatch Alert on its own. For more information, please refer to KBA 3330413 - Ad-hoc EarlyWatch Alert in SAP Focused Run. There are no other methods available to request EWA on demand. 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: How to disable object page table variant management/p...
entry.content:
Hi,

Variant management and the remembering of filters applied on refresh are actually two different things. Variants allow savings/restore of the state at a later point of time even after the current session is over.

The "remembering of filters" is a session specific state persistence (called appstate) and is an implicit handling in Fiori elements that cannot be switched off. You can find more information in this page. 

This appstate handling allows Fiori elements to ensure that the user is brought back to the same state post refresh / post back navigation after an away navigation. It also helps to save the state beyond session when using for example, "Save as Tile" or "Send email" or "Share via MS Teams".

Best Regards,

    Sandeep

entry.title: Re: How to disable object page table variant management/p...

entry.title: Re: Dependency type Sel. condition not allowed for this object Message no. CU027
entry.content: The AVC is a mixture of the new AVC engine and the old VC engine. In a multi-level configuration model (e.g. nested KMAT) dependencies on the profile, characteristics and values need to have processing mode AVC, whereas dependencies on BOM items/routings (like selection conditions) need to have processing mode classic. Here is the online document for your reference: High and Low-Level Configuration | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: saplyphtesttest create a alv
entry.content:
プログラムの解説

  1. PARAMETERS:

    • p_id：必須入力のIDパラメータ。
    • p_name：名前パラメータ。
    • p_date：日付パラメータ。
    • p_time：時間パラメータ。
    • p_list：リストボックスパラメータ。

  2. SELECT-OPTIONS:

    • s_id：IDの範囲選択オプション。
    • s_date：日付の範囲選択オプション。

  3. チェックボックス:

    • p_check：チェックボックスパラメータ。

  4. ラジオボタン:

    • p_radio1, p_radio2, p_radio3：ラジオボタンパラメータ。

  5. リストボックス:

    • リストボックスの値を初期化するために、INITIALIZATION イベントで VRM_SET_VALUES を使用しています。

  6. データ選択と表示:

    • SELECT ステートメントでデータを選択し、WRITE ステートメントで結果を表示しています。
    • チェックボックスの状態、選択されたラジオボタン、リストボックスの選択値も表示しています。

entry.title: Temu.com $100 Off Coupon {act200019}or{acp856709} Code Only New And And Old User
entry.content:
Temu, the popular online shopping platform, is offering an exclusive coupon code that provides a $100 discount for both new and existing customers. The coupon code "act200019" or "acp856709" can be used by anyone looking to save big on their purchases at Temu.com.

For businesses operating in Brazil, navigating the complexities of tax regulations can be a challenge. A crucial aspect of this is complying with electronic invoicing, mandated by the Nota Fiscal Eletronica (NF-e) system. This article explores SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica, a solution designed to simplify NF-e compliance for SAP users.

Understanding NF-e
NF-e refers to the electronic invoice format used in Brazil for transactions involving goods. It eliminates paper invoices, promoting tax efficiency and streamlining the invoicing process. Companies must generate NF-e in a specific XML format, containing details like buyer and seller information, product specifics, and tax data.

SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica: A Helping Hand
SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica is a software solution offered by SAP that aids businesses in complying with NF-e regulations. It integrates seamlessly with existing SAP systems, automating manual tasks and ensuring accurate invoice generation.

Key Benefits of SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica
Enhanced Compliance: The solution automates NF-e generation in the mandated XML format, minimizing errors and ensuring compliance with Brazilian tax authorities (SEFAZ).
Streamlined Processes: Manual data entry for invoices is significantly reduced, saving time and resources.
Improved Accuracy: Automating invoice creation reduces the risk of errors associated with manual data entry.
Secure Communication: Secure and certified communication with SEFAZ is facilitated through the solution.
Efficiency Gains: Faster invoice processing translates to quicker settlements and improved cash flow.
Important Note: Upcoming End of Maintenance"

It's important to be aware that SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica (SAP NFE) is an on-premise solution nearing its end-of-maintenance date (December 31, 2025). SAP recommends exploring alternative solutions to ensure continued compliance with evolving NF-e regulations.

entry.title: Service to service communication of two CAP apps. Called app protected by scope
entry.content:
I have two Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) apps built with node.js, with the following scenario: Application A, bound to appa-xsuaa and to appa-destination (consumer app) Application B, bound toappb-xsuaa (provider app)

Application B has their methods protected by a given scope.

I would like to perform an API call from Application A to Application B, using cds.connect. I have bound the Application A to a destination service instance, and configured a destination of type Oauth2ClientCredentials, pointing to Application B, with their respective service-key credentials.

However, when the consumer app calls the provider app, I am receiving a 403 (forbidden) error. I understand that this is because the technical user from the service key does not have the necessary scope assigned, and the JWT token is generated without any scope.

By reading the documentations, I have found the following guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/application-security-descriptor-configuration-syntax#loio517895a9612241259d6941dbf9ad81cb__section_fm2_wsk_pdb

I have adjusted the xs-security.json from the provider app to grant authority to the consuming app, as follows:

xs-security.json (provider app)

{
  "scopes": [
    {
      "name": "$XSAPPNAME.app-admin",
      "description": "app-admin",
      "grant-as-authority-to-apps" : [ "$XSAPPNAME(application,appa)",
      ]
    }
  ],
  "attributes": [],
  "role-templates": [
    {
      "name": "app-admin",
      "description": "generated",
      "scope-references": [
        "$XSAPPNAME.app-admin"
      ],
      "attribute-references": []
    }
  ]
}

xs-security.json (consumer app)

{
  "scopes": [],
  "attributes": [],
  "role-templates": [],
  "authorities":["$ACCEPT_GRANTED_AUTHORITIES"],
  "foreign-scope-references": ["$ACCEPT_GRANTED_SCOPES"]
}

To make it easier, I have fixed the $XSAPPNAME of the consumer app in the mta file, and set XSAPPNAME to appa. The service module of the app is called appa-srv.

Am I missing something in the correct way for granting scopes for a consumer app through the client credentials grant? Did anyone go through a similar problem in the past?
